name: Chicken with Fennel Red Pepper and Rice
recipeIngredient: 1 large bulb of fennel, fronds removed
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the boneless chicken breasts into even slices. Season with salt and pepper, then sprinkle with dried thyme.
2. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Cook the chicken cutlets for about 2 minutes on each side until browned but not fully cooked through. Transfer to a plate and set aside.
3. Cut fennel into quarters, remove the core, and slice into strips. Cut red pepper into strips as well.
4. In a heavy-bottomed pot or pan, combine rice, chicken broth, and olive oil. Bring to a boil over high heat.
5. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 18 minutes until the rice is tender and has absorbed most of the liquid. Season with salt and pepper, then stir in Parmesan cheese.
6. Return chicken cutlets to serving platter. In the same skillet, add 1 tablespoon olive oil over medium heat.
7. Add fennel and red pepper to the skillet, stirring occasionally for about 3 minutes until vegetables are lightly browned.
8. Pour in white wine, cover, and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
9. To serve, place a layer of rice on serving dishes, top with chicken cutlets, and garnish with fennel and red pepper strips.
